div.p: Thread A's CAS only compares address equality:

The problem: P and P' have the same address but represent completely different logical nodes. CAS cannot distinguish them. The consequence: top now points to Q, which was freed. Any subsequent pop() dereferences freed memory → use-after-free.
With tagged pointers, each modification increments the version counter. Thread A's CAS becomes:
The version mismatch detects that P was recycled. Thread A retries with correct state.
